desc: HTTP requests have no timeout, so an unresponsive server can cause the program to hang indefinitely.
APIClient.fetch returns an error dictionary where callers expect a list, causing process_all to iterate strings and fail with AttributeError.
Successful JSON is used without validating that it is a list of dictionaries, so malformed or unexpected server responses can crash processing.
Null or non-string name, title, or todo title values can cause TypeError during string concatenation.
Constructing URLs with string concatenation produces incorrect paths for base URLs or endpoints with unexpected slash placement.
fetch treats every successful HTTP status other than exactly 200 as an error.
The global session and mutable cache create hidden coupling and potential races when the client is used concurrently.
Catching every Exception and converting it to data obscures failure types and makes errors surface later in unrelated processing code.
